title: caltha palustris subsp. barthei
intro: Caltha palustris subsp. barthei, also known as marsh marigold, is a plant species in the family Ranunculaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its alkaloid content. The medicinal properties of Caltha palustris subsp. barthei are believed to offer health benefits such as wound healing, reduced inflammation, and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
